title: Add prune polish bundle

tasks.title: Add structlog per-event warnings to BOTH prune paths (parity).

evidence_plan.proof_target: Automated red/green tests evidenced by adv_run_test
evidence_plan.rationale: Behavior-visible observability addition. capture_logs assertion verifies the events fire with the right shape. Static check alone cannot confirm the wiring.

tasks.verification: Added structlog per-event warnings to BOTH prune_orphans.py and prune_symbols.py at the three failure-capture sites in each execute branch: prune_refused_symlink, prune_refused_outside_root, prune_unlink_failed. Each event carries a store="orphans"|"symbols" kwarg for filterability. Pattern mirrors index_store.py and index_repo.py. C2 honored: failures[] shape unchanged (log.warning is strictly additive). 6 new capture_logs tests added (3 per file). Full suite 657 passed, ruff clean.

tasks.title: Add `--symbols-dir DIR` flag to `_cmd_gc`, forwarded to the embedded `prune_symbols` sweep.

evidence_plan.proof_target: Automated red/green tests evidenced by adv_run_test
evidence_plan.rationale: CLI behavior change with observable effect (storage_dir forwarding). Test asserts the kwarg reaches prune_symbols.

tasks.verification: Added --symbols-dir DIR flag to _cmd_gc, mirroring existing --cache-dir pattern exactly: help text updated, parser branch added, symbols_dir initialized None alongside cache_dir, forwarded via prune_symbols(dry_run=dry_run, storage_dir=symbols_dir). DONT1 honored: combined-report key set unchanged. 3 new tests added (forwarding, absent-passes-None, help-text). Full suite 657 pass, ruff clean.

tasks.verification: Added CHANGELOG.md entry under '## Unreleased' describing the prune-symbols capability shipped in addPrunePolishBundle (merge 6eafc1d3): CLI subcommand, MCP tool, extended lgrep gc (3 sweeps + new --symbols-dir), LGREP_SYMBOLS_DIR env var, structlog per-event observability. Closes issue #5. Format matches existing v3.1.7/3.1.8 entries. Cross-referenced against shipped code in commit 6eafc1d3.

sources.label: Existing structlog use
sources.summary: Uses module-level structlog.get_logger() and named event calls with structured keyword fields.

sources.label: Existing remote-index logging
sources.summary: Uses the same module-level logger and log.warning(event_name, field=value) pattern.

sources.label: Server logging configuration
sources.summary: Configures WriteLoggerFactory to stderr, not standard-library LoggerFactory.

sources.label: pytest caplog documentation
sources.summary: caplog captures logging records and supports caplog.set_level().

sources.label: structlog standard-library integration
sources.summary: Standard-library record integration requires stdlib LoggerFactory and render_to_log_kwargs-style configuration.

sources.label: structlog testing documentation
sources.summary: capture_logs/LogCapture are supported structured-log assertion mechanisms.

sources.label: GC command implementation
sources.summary: _cmd_gc currently exposes --cache-dir, parses it with Path(...).resolve(), and calls prune_symbols without storage_dir.

sources.label: Prune implementations
sources.summary: prune_orphans and gc_worktree_meta are separate maintenance operations; report returns include a typed failures array.

sources.label: Symbol prune implementation
sources.summary: prune_symbols has an analogous typed report and failures array.

sources.label: Changelog convention
sources.summary: Top entries use a dated version heading and Added/Changed/Fixed sections.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: The proposed module-level structlog logger and warning event call exactly match existing local patterns. The GC symbols-dir option can mirror cache-dir parsing and pass storage_dir=... to prune_symbols. Do not use caplog: configured production structlog writes directly to stderr rather than creating stdlib LogRecords. Use structlog.testing.capture_logs and assert the structured event dictionary. Preserve report construction exactly; emit an event after the report is computed, not by appending data to failures[]. store is a new, unambiguous field name; no repository evidence favors path_kind or target_store.

validation.notes: Design is compatible except a caplog.records assertion would not verify current WriteLoggerFactory output. Scope should explicitly decide whether gc_worktree_meta, the third sweep in lgrep gc, also merits a completion event.

tradeoffs: Structured-log tests use structlog's testing capture rather than pytest's generic caplog fixture.
Logging only the two prune functions leaves gc_worktree_meta without an equivalent maintenance completion event; logging every internal branch would add noise.

alternatives_considered.rationale: Current server configuration uses structlog.WriteLoggerFactory to stderr, while caplog captures standard-library LogRecords; official structlog docs show stdlib LoggerFactory integration is required for that path.

alternatives_considered.option: Reconfigure production logging to stdlib solely for tests
alternatives_considered.rationale: Unnecessary wider logging architecture change; structlog supplies capture_logs for structured-event tests.

alternatives_considered.option: Emit events from every classification and failure branch
alternatives_considered.rationale: Higher-volume, noisier logs; one completion/summary event per maintenance operation is simpler.

architecture_judgement.recommendation: Ship with caveats: add summary events after prune reports are computed, test them with structlog.testing.capture_logs, mirror --cache-dir exactly for --symbols-dir and pass it only to prune_symbols, and keep failures[] untouched.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Revise test design from caplog to structlog.testing.capture_logs. Otherwise implement the lightweight bundle as designed; consider adding a summary event for gc_worktree_meta only if the change scope defines all three GC sweeps as observability targets.
follow_ups: Packet omitted SCOPE KEY, IN_SCOPE, OUT_OF_SCOPE, DONE_WHEN, STOP_WHEN, and VERIFICATION anchors; report scope key is a transport placeholder, not inferred contract scope.
No project convention exists for store, target_store, or path_kind keyword fields; store='orphans'|'symbols' is clear and minimally scoped.

documents.proposal:
# Polish bundle: prune observability + gc ergonomics + CHANGELOG

## Problem
Three small follow-up items surfaced during `addSymbolStorePrune` review and deferred to a separate change:

1. **Observability gap (advisory)** — both `prune_orphans` and `prune_symbols` capture refused-symlink / refused-out-of-root / per-entry unlink failures in their respective `failures[]` arrays but emit no `structlog` events. Ops running the MCP server see only the JSON report; the daemon's structured logs carry no per-event trace of security-relevant refusals.
2. **CLI ergonomics gap** — `lgrep gc` accepts `--cache-dir DIR` (semantic cache override) but has no equivalent override for the symbol store. Operators who relocated their symbol store via `LGREP_SYMBOLS_DIR` must invoke `lgrep prune-symbols` separately rather than getting both sweeps in one gc pass.
3. **Release-prep docs gap** — `CHANGELOG.md` has no entry for the new `prune-symbols` capability shipped in `addSymbolStorePrune` (now on `main` as merge `6eafc1d3`).

## Scope
- Add structured `log.warning` events alongside existing `failures[]` capture in both prune paths (parity).
- Add `--symbols-dir DIR` flag to `_cmd_gc`, forwarded to the embedded `prune_symbols` call.
- Add a CHANGELOG entry under a new version documenting the new capability.

## Out of scope
- scan→unlink race mutex (KD7) — remains deferred; the proper fix covers `IndexStore.save` + both prune paths in one dedicated change. Atomic temp+rename keeps the practical window tiny.
- New env vars, new commands, new spec capabilities.
- Behavior changes to existing `prune-orphans`, `prune_symbols`, `invalidate_cache`, or `lgrep gc` existing keys.

## Acceptance criteria
- AC1: Refused-symlink, refused-out-of-root, and per-entry unlink-failure events in both `prune_orphans` and `prune_symbols` emit `log.warning` with the path and reason. Existing `failures[]` capture unchanged.
- AC2: `lgrep gc --symbols-dir DIR` forwards the override to `prune_symbols`. Help text lists the new flag.
- AC3: `CHANGELOG.md` entry exists, dated, describing the new capability surfaces.
- AC4: Existing tests pass; new tests cover the structlog emission and the gc symbols-dir forwarding.

## Constraints
- C1 (parity): structlog emission pattern must match between prune_orphans and prune_symbols; no divergence.
- C2 (no behavior change): the `failures[]` array shape and content stays identical; structlog events are additional, not replacement.
- DONT1: do not change `lgrep gc` combined-report key set (`prune_orphans`, `gc_worktree_meta`, `prune_symbols` preserved).
- DONT2: do not change existing `--cache-dir` semantics in gc.

## Avoidances
- DONT3: do not add the scan→unlink mutex here (KD7 defer).
- DONT4: do not rewrite the existing prune-symbols/prune-orphans core logic.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement: addPrunePolishBundle

## Objectives
1. **Observability parity** — security-relevant refusal events in both prune paths emit `structlog` warnings alongside existing `failures[]` capture. Ops get per-event visibility in daemon logs without parsing JSON reports.
2. **gc ergonomics** — `lgrep gc` accepts `--symbols-dir DIR` and forwards to the embedded `prune_symbols` sweep, matching the existing `--cache-dir` pattern.
3. **Release-docs currency** — `CHANGELOG.md` has an entry for the prune-symbols capability shipped in `addPrunePolishBundle` (currently unreleased on `main` post v3.1.8).

## Success Criteria
- SC1: Both `prune_orphans` and `prune_symbols` emit `log.warning` events on refused-symlink, refused-out-of-root, and per-entry unlink-failure. Events include path and reason/error.
- SC2: `lgrep gc --symbols-dir DIR` forwards the override to `prune_symbols`; help text lists the flag.
- SC3: `CHANGELOG.md` carries a dated entry describing the new capability (CLI subcommand + MCP tool + extended gc).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC1: For each of {refused-symlink, refused-out-of-root, per-entry-unlink-failure} in BOTH `prune_orphans` and `prune_symbols`, a `log.warning` event is emitted with at minimum `{path, reason|error}` fields. Existing `failures[]` capture unchanged (additive only).
- AC2: `lgrep gc [--symbols-dir DIR]` parses the flag and forwards to `prune_symbols(storage_dir=...)`. The combined report's `prune_symbols` key reflects the override. Help text lists the flag.
- AC3: `CHANGELOG.md` has a new entry (Unreleased or version-dated) describing the prune-symbols surfaces: CLI `lgrep prune-symbols` subcommand, MCP `prune_symbols` tool, extended `lgrep gc` (3 sweeps). Entry is consistent with the existing format.
- AC4: All existing tests pass; new tests cover (a) structlog emission via caplog fixture, (b) gc --symbols-dir forwarding via existing test pattern.

## Constraints
- C1 (parity): structlog emission pattern must match between `prune_orphans` and `prune_symbols` — same event-name conventions, same kwarg shape. No divergence.
- C2 (additive only): `failures[]` array shape and content stays identical. Structlog events are additional, not replacement.
- C3 (style conformance): structlog usage follows the established pattern (`log = structlog.get_logger()` at module top; `log.warning("snake_case_event", kw=val)` calls).
- C4 (no new env vars): no new env knobs introduced.

## Avoidances
- DONT1: do not change `lgrep gc` combined-report key set (`prune_orphans`, `gc_worktree_meta`, `prune_symbols` preserved).
- DONT2: do not change existing `--cache-dir` semantics or help text in gc.
- DONT3: do not add the scan→unlink mutex here (KD7 deferred — separate change if pursued).
- DONT4: do not rewrite the existing prune-symbols/prune-orphans core logic; only add observability alongside existing capture.
- DONT5: do not change `lgrep prune-symbols` standalone CLI surface (it already has `--storage-dir`).

## Out of Scope
- OOS1: scan→unlink race mutex (KD7) — dedicated change if pursued.
- OOS2: any new MCP tool, CLI subcommand, or env var.
- OOS3: behavior change to existing prune logic, classification, or guards.
- OOS4: spec-delta modifications to `lgrepSymbolStoreLifecycle` capability (the new capability already ships the right invariants; this change is polish only).
documents.design:
# Design: addPrunePolishBundle

## Architecture
No new modules, no new surfaces. Three independent edits to existing files, each mirroring an established pattern:

### 1. Structlog emission in prune paths (AC1)

**Pattern source**: `src/lgrep/storage/index_store.py:23,161,194,196,303,315` and `src/lgrep/tools/index_repo.py:18,97,118,146`.

Both `prune_orphans.py` and `prune_symbols.py` get:
- `import structlog` + `log = structlog.get_logger()` at module top (after existing imports)
- `log.warning(...)` calls at the three existing `failures.append(...)` sites:
  - Refused symlink at delete time
  - Refused out-of-root path at delete time
  - Per-entry unlink OSError

**Event names** (snake_case, descriptive, prefixed with `prune_` for filterability):
- `prune_refused_symlink` — kwargs: `{path, store: "orphans"|"symbols"}`
- `prune_refused_outside_root` — kwargs: `{path, store}`
- `prune_unlink_failed` — kwargs: `{path, store, error}`

The `store` kwarg lets ops filter prune events by store kind without parsing the call site. Existing `failures[]` capture remains identical (additive only per C2).

### 2. gc --symbols-dir flag (AC2)

**Pattern source**: existing `--cache-dir` flag in `_cmd_gc` at `src/lgrep/cli.py:446-448`.

Add parallel `--symbols-dir DIR` parsing in the same `while i < len(args)` loop. Forward via `prune_symbols(dry_run=dry_run, storage_dir=symbols_dir)`. Update help text to list the new flag. Combined-report key set unchanged (DONT1).

### 3. CHANGELOG entry (AC3)

**Pattern source**: existing v3.1.7 / v3.1.8 entries in `CHANGELOG.md`.

Add a new top entry under `## Unreleased` (or version-dated if project prefers) describing:
- New CLI subcommand `lgrep prune-symbols`
- New MCP tool `prune_symbols`
- Extended `lgrep gc` (now 3 sweeps)
- New env var `LGREP_SYMBOLS_DIR`
- Default dry-run on every surface; transport coercion on shared HTTP

## LBP decisions
- **Why `store` kwarg on every event?** Locality + filterability. Without it, ops grepping daemon logs cannot distinguish prune_orphans events from prune_symbols events without call-site context. With it, `grep prune_refused_symlink | jq 'select(.store=="symbols")'` works.
- **Why not introduce a shared `_log_prune_failure()` helper?** Two modules, three call sites each = six total. A helper would add indirection for a one-liner. Keep the `log.warning(...)` inline next to the existing `failures.append(...)` for locality (P04).
- **Why Unreleased vs version-dated CHANGELOG?** Project auto-releases via conventional commits; the bump happens at release time. `## Unreleased` is the conventional slot for not-yet-released main-branch work.
- **Why is `gc_worktree_meta` excluded from structlog emission?** It's a maintenance sweep (alias-entry cleanup) with a different failure shape, not a "prune path" in the same sense. AC1 explicitly scopes to `prune_orphans` and `prune_symbols` only. gc_worktree_meta could get its own observability in a separate change if needed.

## Test fixture (validator caveat resolved)

Project structlog config uses `WriteLoggerFactory` (writes to stderr directly), NOT stdlib `LogRecord`s (per `src/lgrep/server/bootstrap.py`). This means pytest's `caplog.records` will NOT capture events from `log.warning(...)` calls.

**Use `structlog.testing.capture_logs` instead:**
```python
from structlog.testing import capture_logs

def test_prune_symbols_warns_on_refused_symlink(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    # ... set up symlink refusal scenario ...
    with capture_logs() as cap_logs:
        prune_symbols(storage_dir=storage, dry_run=False)
    matching = [e for e in cap_logs if e["event"] == "prune_refused_symlink" and e["store"] == "symbols"]
    assert len(matching) == 1
```

This is the canonical structlog test pattern (per [structlog testing docs](https://www.structlog.org/en/stable/testing.html)). `capture_logs` temporarily replaces the configured processors and captures events as dicts.

## Implementation strategy
Three independent tasks, no dependencies. Can execute sequentially or in parallel. Each task is self-contained:
- Task A (structlog): edits `prune_orphans.py` + `prune_symbols.py`; tests use `structlog.testing.capture_logs`
- Task B (gc flag): edits `cli.py`; tests use existing `_cmd_gc` test pattern
- Task C (CHANGELOG): edits `CHANGELOG.md`; no tests (docs)

## Risks
- **Log volume**: prune operations over large stores with many refusals could emit many warnings. Mitigation: warnings only fire on refusals (not on every examined entry), so volume is bounded by refusal count, not store size.
- **capture_logs context manager**: must wrap the call site exactly; events outside the `with` block are not captured. Tests must include the prune invocation inside the `with` block.

## Verification
- `uv run pytest tests/` — full suite must pass (was 648 after `addPrunePolishBundle`; expect +N new tests)
- `uv run ruff check .` — clean
- Manual: `lgrep gc --symbols-dir /tmp/test-symbols --dry-run` should produce a combined report with `prune_symbols` reflecting the override
- Manual: induce a symlink refusal and confirm `log.warning` fires (covered by automated test via `capture_logs`)

## Validator verdict
`ship_with_caveats` from adv-researcher (attempt 1). Caveat was the caplog/capture_logs distinction; resolved in this revision by specifying `structlog.testing.capture_logs` as the test fixture. All other aspects (pattern compatibility, kwarg shape, gc flag mirror, CHANGELOG format) confirmed pass.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

## Outcome
Three small follow-up items from `addPrunePolishBundle` review are now implemented: observability parity (structlog events in both prune paths), CLI ergonomics (`lgrep gc --symbols-dir DIR`), and CHANGELOG entry for the previously-shipped prune-symbols capability. No behavior change to existing surfaces.

## Why It Matters
Multi-agent hosts running the lgrep MCP daemon get structured-log visibility into security-relevant prune refusals (symlinks, out-of-root paths, unlink failures) without parsing JSON reports. Operators with relocated symbol stores can override via gc in one pass instead of invoking `prune-symbols` separately. CHANGELOG now documents the capability that shipped in `addPrunePolishBundle` (commit `6eafc1d3`).

## Verdict
APPROVED — 0 blockers, 0 issues. Inline review (small polish change; scanner fan-out not warranted for mirror-existing-pattern work).

## What Was Built
1. **Structlog parity** (`src/lgrep/tools/prune_orphans.py`, `src/lgrep/tools/prune_symbols.py`) — `log.warning(...)` events at all 3 failure-capture sites in both execute branches. Events: `prune_refused_symlink`, `prune_refused_outside_root`, `prune_unlink_failed`. Each carries a `store: "orphans"|"symbols"` kwarg for filterability. Pattern mirrors `index_store.py` and `tools/index_repo.py`. Existing `failures[]` capture unchanged (strictly additive per C2).
2. **gc --symbols-dir flag** (`src/lgrep/cli.py`) — new `--symbols-dir DIR` parsed identically to existing `--cache-dir`, forwarded via `prune_symbols(storage_dir=symbols_dir)`. Help text updated. Combined-report key set unchanged (DONT1).
3. **CHANGELOG entry** (`CHANGELOG.md`) — new `## Unreleased` section with 5 bullets covering: CLI subcommand, MCP tool, extended gc, LGREP_SYMBOLS_DIR env var, structlog observability. Closes issue #5.

## What Was Verified
- Verdict: APPROVED (inline review, 0 blockers / 0 issues).
- Tests: `uv run pytest tests/` → **657 passed, 0 failures** (was 648 baseline; +9 new: 6 capture_logs + 3 gc forwarding). `uv run ruff check .` → clean.

- Tests: `tests/test_prune_orphans.py` (+3 capture_logs), `tests/test_prune_symbols.py` (+3 capture_logs), `tests/test_cli.py::TestGcSymbolsDir` (+3 forwarding/help/absent).
- Docs: `CHANGELOG.md`.

- Commit: `bdbd849` (single checkpoint covering all 3 tasks).

# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-07-21T04:50:00.000Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SC1 | success_criterion | Both `prune_orphans` and `prune_symbols` emit `log.warning` events on refused-symlink, refused-out-of-root, and per-entry unlink-failure. Events include path and reason/error. | pass | Both prune_orphans.py and prune_symbols.py emit log.warning at all 3 failure-capture sites. 6 capture_logs tests pass (3 per file). Events: prune_refused_symlink, prune_refused_outside_root, prune_unlink_failed. store kwarg distinguishes orphans/symbols. |
| SC2 | success_criterion | `lgrep gc --symbols-dir DIR` forwards the override to `prune_symbols`; help text lists the flag. | pass | _cmd_gc --symbols-dir DIR parses and forwards via prune_symbols(storage_dir=symbols_dir). test_gc_symbols_dir_forwarded_to_prune_symbols verifies Path resolution. |
| SC3 | success_criterion | `CHANGELOG.md` carries a dated entry describing the new capability (CLI subcommand + MCP tool + extended gc). | pass | CHANGELOG.md has '## Unreleased' entry describing CLI subcommand, MCP tool, extended gc, LGREP_SYMBOLS_DIR, structlog observability. Format matches existing v3.1.7/3.1.8. |
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | For each of {refused-symlink, refused-out-of-root, per-entry-unlink-failure} in BOTH `prune_orphans` and `prune_symbols`, a `log.warning` event is emitted with at minimum `{path, reason|error}` fields. Existing `failures[]` capture unchanged (additive only). | pass | prune_orphans.py:333,347,364,389 + prune_symbols.py:378,392,409,435 — log.warning at all 3 failure sites in both files. tests/test_prune_orphans.py + tests/test_prune_symbols.py: 6 new capture_logs tests pass. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | `lgrep gc [--symbols-dir DIR]` parses the flag and forwards to `prune_symbols(storage_dir=...)`. The combined report's `prune_symbols` key reflects the override. Help text lists the flag. | pass | cli.py:419,439,451-453,465 — help text, parser, forwarding. tests/test_cli.py::TestGcSymbolsDir: 3 tests (forwarding, absent-passes-None, help-text). |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | `CHANGELOG.md` has a new entry (Unreleased or version-dated) describing the prune-symbols surfaces: CLI `lgrep prune-symbols` subcommand, MCP `prune_symbols` tool, extended `lgrep gc` (3 sweeps). Entry is consistent with the existing format. | pass | CHANGELOG.md:1-16 — new '## Unreleased' entry with all 5 bullet points (CLI subcommand, MCP tool, gc extension, env var, structlog). Cross-referenced against shipped code 6eafc1d3. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | All existing tests pass; new tests cover (a) structlog emission via caplog fixture, (b) gc --symbols-dir forwarding via existing test pattern. | pass | Full suite 657 passed (was 648; +9 new: 6 capture_logs + 3 gc forwarding). Ruff clean. TDD: structlog tests written first then implementation added (existing tests still green during transition). |
| C1 | constraint | C1 (parity): structlog emission pattern must match between `prune_orphans` and `prune_symbols` — same event-name conventions, same kwarg shape. No divergence. | respected | Event names identical between files: prune_refused_symlink, prune_refused_outside_root, prune_unlink_failed. Kwargs shape identical (path, store, [error]). Only store value differs ('orphans' vs 'symbols'). |
| C2 | constraint | C2 (additive only): `failures[]` array shape and content stays identical. Structlog events are additional, not replacement. | respected | git diff shows log.warning calls are ADDED alongside existing failures.append calls; no failures.append payload modified. Existing failure tests still pass unchanged. |
| C3 | constraint | C3 (style conformance): structlog usage follows the established pattern (`log = structlog.get_logger()` at module top; `log.warning("snake_case_event", kw=val)` calls). | respected | import structlog + log = structlog.get_logger() pattern matches index_store.py:23-25 and tools/index_repo.py:15-18. Event names follow snake_case convention with descriptive kwargs. |
| C4 | constraint | C4 (no new env vars): no new env knobs introduced. | respected | git diff main..HEAD -- src/lgrep/ shows no new os.environ references. No new env knobs. |
| DONT1 | avoidance | do not change `lgrep gc` combined-report key set (`prune_orphans`, `gc_worktree_meta`, `prune_symbols` preserved). | respected | cli.py:466-470 combined dict still has exactly {prune_orphans, gc_worktree_meta, prune_symbols} keys. test_gc_combined_report_includes_prune_symbols still passes. |
| DONT2 | avoidance | do not change existing `--cache-dir` semantics or help text in gc. | respected | cli.py --cache-dir help text and parser unchanged. Only --symbols-dir added. |
| DONT3 | avoidance | do not add the scan→unlink mutex here (KD7 deferred — separate change if pursued). | respected | No fcntl/flock/lock-file imports added. KD7 deferred. |
| DONT4 | avoidance | do not rewrite the existing prune-symbols/prune-orphans core logic; only add observability alongside existing capture. | respected | git diff shows only log.warning calls added to prune_orphans.py and prune_symbols.py. No classification, guard, or return-shape changes. |
| DONT5 | avoidance | do not change `lgrep prune-symbols` standalone CLI surface (it already has `--storage-dir`). | respected | _cmd_prune_symbols standalone CLI surface unchanged (still --execute/--dry-run/--storage-dir). Only _cmd_gc got the new --symbols-dir flag. |
